content:
🚀 token-dashboard | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with local, detailed analytics and cost insights into their Claude Code AI interactions, enabling them to identify and optimize expensive prompts and wasteful usage patterns.
✨ Key Features: • Local data processing: No data leaves the user's machine. • Per-prompt cost analytics. • Tool/file heatmaps. • Subagent attribution. • Cache analytics.
📖 Summary: Token Dashboard is a local application that analyzes JSONL transcripts generated by Claude Code. It provides insights into token usage, costs, and patterns, helping users understand and optimize their AI interactions without sending any data off their machine.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 npxskillui | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To reverse-engineer existing design systems from any source (web, local, repo) and make them readily usable by AI code generation tools like Claude for rapid UI development.
✨ Key Features: • Extracts design systems (colors, typography, spacing, components, animations, screenshots) from URLs, local directories, or Git repositories.
📖 Summary: SkillUI is a command-line interface tool that analyzes websites, local codebases, or Git repositories to extract their complete design systems. It packages this information into a format that AI models like Claude can directly use to generate UI code, enabling rapid UI development based on existing design languages.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 kittyfarm | Swift
🎯 Primary Use Case: Running and managing multiple iOS Simulators and Android Emulators concurrently on a Mac for efficient mobile app testing and development.
✨ Key Features: • Live display of iOS Simulators and Android Emulators in a single window • Synchronized touch input across multiple devices • Device bezels rendered from official assets • Build & Play functionality for parallel deployment • Drag-and-drop device reordering
📖 Summary: KittyFarm is a macOS application designed to streamline mobile development workflows by allowing developers to run and control multiple iOS Simulators and Android Emulators simultaneously within a single window. It offers features like synchronized touch input, parallel deployment, and proportional device scaling.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 allure-notifications | Java
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating the distribution of test execution reports to communication platforms.
✨ Key Features: • Automatic test result notifications • Support for multiple messengers (Telegram, Slack, Mattermost, Discord, etc.) • Generates a pie chart visualization of test statistics • Includes a link to the Allure report/build • Configurable via JSON file
📖 Summary: Allure Notifications is a Java library that automatically sends test execution results to various messengers. It generates a pie chart from the test statistics and includes a link to the build report.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 moss | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling developers to integrate a secure, decentralized P2P mesh networking capability into applications written in various languages through a C-shared library interface.
✨ Key Features: • Embeddable P2P mesh core • Exported as C-shared library via CGO • Tracker-based bootstrapping (BEP 15 UDP, BEP 3 HTTP) • Encrypted peer transport (Noise XX) • Topic-based pub/sub routing (GRAFT/PRUNE)
📖 Summary: Moss is an embeddable P2P mesh networking core written in Go, exported as a C-shared library for cross-language integration. It provides secure, topic-based pub/sub messaging with tracker-based bootstrapping and NAT traversal capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
